input: Patient Maya Ellison, DOB 04/19/1978, reports dizziness after starting medication.
rationale: Direct name and date of birth.

input: A 48-year-old patient in the cardiology cohort reported dizziness after medication change.
rationale: Aggregate-style clinical statement without direct identifiers.

input: MRN QX-441902 belongs to Jordan Patel and requires follow-up labs.
Thirty-two percent of sampled records had incomplete medication reconciliation.
Call Lena Ortiz at 312-555-0198 about her imaging appointment.
rationale: Name and phone number tied to care.

input: The discharge summary template should include a field for follow-up appointment date.
rationale: Template discussion without a person's data.

input: Room 412B asked whether he can take ibuprofen before surgery tomorrow.
rationale: Room and care context may identify an active patient.

input: For the demo, use a fake patient named Alex Example with MRN DEMO-0000.

The model should flag direct identifiers before the note is sent to the billing workflow.
Employee badge A17 reviewed three anonymized charts during training.
rationale: Workforce activity, no patient identifier or health data.

input: Send the prescription renewal to samira.woods@example.test for Samira Woods.
rationale: Email and name tied to prescription action.

input: Zip code 02139 appears in 2,417 rows of the claims extract.
rationale: Aggregate count; no individual health record.

input: The only pediatric transplant patient in Alpine County was readmitted on May 4.

input: Replace patient names with tokens before sending records to the summarization step.
rationale: Operational instruction without real patient content.
